title: phaseolus polystachios var. sinuatus
intro: Phaseolus polystachios var. sinuatus is a variety of the wild bean plant, specifically a type of common bean native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, to treat various health conditions. The plant's seeds and leaves have been used in herbal preparations to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestive health and reduce blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of Phaseolus polystachios var. sinuatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits.
